channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Showdown Challenges & Rewards
item.content:encoded:
A slew of new content is available in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2, including new Showdown challenges to earn in-game rewards, and a global leaderboard with physical prizes available.

Fortnite‘s Showdown challenges are geared around the theme of Chapter 7 Season 2, which sees The Foundation, a returning member of The Seven, face off against the Ice King.

For all the details on the Showdown Weekly Challenges, the available rewards, and the physical rewards you can earn, Insider Gaming has you covered.

How to Sign Up For Fortnite Showdown Rewards

While some in-game rewards are earned through progression, others require signing up via the official Fortnite Showdown website to earn additional rewards.

After selecting Team Ice King or Team Foundation, head to the Fortnite Showdown website and accept the terms and conditions to sign up for additional rewards.

Rewards range from additional in-game rewards, earned by completing special weekly challenges, to physical rewards including Nvidia Graphics Cards and merchandise.

All Showdown Rewards & Challenges, Listed

Various in-game rewards are available in Fortnite Showdown during Chapter 7 Season 2, distributed after you complete specific challenges.

The event period is set to last for the opening four weeks of the season, with new challenges dropping, though it’s not clear how long after the drop of the final weekly challenge they will remain available.

  • More Insider Gaming: Should You Choose Team Foundation or Team Ice King?

Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 is due to end on June 5, but the rewards may disappear before that date rolls around.

The full list of Showdown Challenges and rewards is shown in the table below. This will be updated weekly when new challenges are revealed.

All Showdown Physical Rewards & How to Earn

The top players in the world will receive physical rewards during Fortnite’s Showdown event, though there are some restrictions.

The Global Leaderboard Challenge kicks off on March 19, alongside the new season launch, and will remain available until April 16 at 23:59 ET.

  • More Insider Gaming: All New & Unvaulted Weapons in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2

To be eligible for rewards, players must have a valid Epic Games account and have selected Team Ice King or Team Foundation to participate in the challenge.

All of the available physical rewards for the Fortnite Showdown event and the requirements to earn them are shown in the table below.

Which reward have you got your eyes on in the Fortnite Showdown event? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, find out how to get the Launch Week Bonus, and check out details of the Star Wars items available in Creative.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fortnite-chapter-7-season-2-showdown-challenges-rewards/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ESL One Birmingham 2026 Tournament Guide—Schedule, Format, and Teams
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming looks forward to ESL One Birmingham 2026. The $1,000,000 tournament sees Tier 1 Dota 2 return to the UK for the first time since ESL One Birmingham 2024, which saw Team Falcons prevail in front of a packed Birmingham crowd.

The event is part of the DreamHack Birmingham festival, which features several esports tournaments, including Call of Duty Major II.

ESL One Birmingham Schedule and Format

ESL One Birmingham schedule:

  • March 22-29
  • Arena Playoffs: March 27-29

The format follows the previous ESL One structure:

Group Stage:

  • Two groups of eight
  • Teams play Best of 2 series in a round-robin format
  • Top two by series record advance to the Playoffs Upper Bracket
  • 3rd/4th teams advance to Playoffs Lower Bracket
  • 5th-8th teams are eliminated
  • Tierbreaker Bo3 for two teams with equal records for consequentual placements, Bo1 round-robin for three or more teams.

Playoffs:

  • Eight-team double-elimination bracket
  • All matches Best of 3, except Bo5 Grand Final

ESL One Birmingham 2026 Teams

Group A

  • BetBoom
  • GamerLegion
  • MOUZ
  • Parivision
  • REKONIX
  • Yandex
  • Tundra
  • Yakult Brothers

Group B

  • Aurora
  • Nigma Galaxy
  • OG
  • paiN
  • Falcons
  • Spirit
  • Virtus.pro
  • Xtreme Gaming

Prize Pool

Prize pool: $1,000,000
Club Reward: $250,000
First Place: $250,000 + $40,000 Club Reward

ESL One Birmingham 2026 Location

Birmingham, United Kingdom

Arena playoffs: bp pulse LIVE, Birmingham

How to watch ESL One Birmingham 2026

You can follow ESL One Birmingham through ESL’s dedicated Twitch and YouTube Dota 2 channels:

  • ESL Dota 2 Twitch
  • ESL Dota 2 YouTube

ESL One Birmingham 2026 Predictions

Tier 1 Dota 2 has seen five different tournament winners since The International 2025, with Tundra, Falcons, Mouz, Yandex, and Liquid all securing Tier 1 trophies in the 2025/26 season.

Of those five, Yandex are the team in form coming into ESL Birmingham with their PGL Wallachia Season 7 victory backing up their DreamLeague Season 27 triumph.

The rising roster won Wallachia despite playing with Dmitry ‘DM’ Dorokhin as a stand-in, an ominous sign for their opposition in Birmingham.

The field will not have to worry about PGL Wallachia Season 7 runners-up, Liquid, who failed to qualify for the event after a 5th-6th place finish in the regional qualifier.

Tundra have secured the most trophies of the five event winners, winning BLAST Slam IV, BLAST Slam V, and DreamLeague Season 28, and will now look to replicate that on stage.

The roster will be extra motivated for the event, as it is the home event of their Position 4 player, Matthew ‘Ari’ Walker. The Brit has spent the majority of his career at a far distance from trophies, but in Tundra, he is placed to capture glory at ESL One Birmingham 2026.

Predicted Winner: Tundra Esports

Who do you think will come out on top at ESL One Birmingham? You can discuss that on our community Discord server!

See how the $1.7m Prize Pool at DreamHack Birmingham is shared throughout its esport competitions.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/esl-one-birmingham-2026-tournament-guide/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fortnite Creators Can Soon Make Star Wars Battlefront-Style Islands
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite’s upcoming May 2026 update will let players create Star Wars-inspired islands in UEFN and Creative.

Chapter 7 Season 2 for Fortnite arrives today, March 19, bringing a showdown between Team Foundation and Team Ice King, and letting players choose their own rewards. But that’s for the battle royale part of the game; another update has now been revealed for the Creative and UEFN modes.

Fortnite Players Can Create Star Wars Islands from May 2026

Details of the Fortnite Ecosystem update v40.00 have been shared, revealing that the ability to create Star Wars islands arrives on May 1, 2026. The developers have provided some island templates to give creators starting points for their experiences, each featuring a different set of assets and setup elements. Check out all the Fortnite Star Wars Islands starter templates below:

  • Tatooine-Inspired Starter Island: Follow the twin suns to the Tatooine-inspired town. Create your next desert theme game with this starter island.
  • Nevarro-Inspired Starter Island: Explore the Nevarro-inspired city. Use the volcanic landscape to start building your next adventure.
  • Hoth-Inspired Starter Island: Get started building with this Hoth-inspired starter island. Take in the icy vista landscape and hidden bases as inspiration for your next experience.
  • Space-Starter Island: Start the adventure in space to build out your galaxy. This template includes an example setup for a hyperspace effect.

Players also have gameplay learning templates to check out that explain how to create custom lightsabers and Force powers using Scene Graph and Verse, how to build large-scale team combat experiences, and how to build narrative-driven and role-playing experiences.

Star Wars Assets That Will Be Included in Fortnite Creative

Players will need a lot of assets to deck out their Star Wars islands in Fortnite. You have an option to choose from iconic characters that we have already seen in the game, including Luke Skywalker, Darth Vader, Boba Fett, Chewbacca, The Mandalorian, Princess Leia, Rebel Alliance characters, plus civilians, Stormtroopers, Jawas, rideable Tauntauns, and more.

Regarding weapons, players have 25 of them to choose from in the Star Wars universe, including the Mandalorian Jetpack. Moreover, players can use themed assets from First Order Base, Nevarro City, Outpost Enclave, Resistance Base, Death Star interior, and Republic Outpost. There are also biome assets to choose from to make the islands more immersive, including desert, snow, and volcanic assets.

Fortnite Star Wars Hero Device, Vehicles, and Music

Moving forward, players will have a Hero Device that transforms them into heroes and villains from the Star Wars universe, including Darth Vader, The Mandalorian, Rey Skywalker, Chewbacca, Emperor Palpatine, and Leia Organa, each with unique powers and abilities. A selection of officially licensed Star Wars music will also be available for a limited time. Lastly, vehicles include AATs, N-1 Starfighters, X-wings, and TIE fighters.

Lightsabers, Force Powers, and Devices

Star Wars Islands in Fortnite will feature new Force powers with special gameplay abilities, including protective barriers, healing allies, mind-tricking opponents, casting distractions, and enemy detection. Players can also design custom lightsabers by changing hilts, blade styles, and blade colors.

Regarding devices, Guard Device has been updated to include character-themed hire and dismiss dialogue. Other updated devices include the following changes:

  • Disguise Devices: Now includes a new array of Star Wars characters, including Stormtroopers and Rebel Alliance characters, so you can put your players right in the heart of battle.
  • Conversation Devices: Now features additional weightings for randomized dialogue, so your characters can have more variety.
  • Capture Area Devices: Now includes a neutralize event and the option to hide the device.
  • Player Spawner Devices: Verse implementation of Respawn The Player, where you can set it to the target player or the player and a list of devices to choose the best spawn point.
  • Team Settings and Inventory Devices: Also includes the Verse implementation of Respawn The Player.

In other news, Fortnite has teased the arrival of Italian Brainrot characters. Additionally, here’s a guide on whether you should choose Team Ice King or Team Foundation in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2. What are your thoughts on Fortnite allowing players to publish Star Wars-style islands? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Meta Horizon Worlds being retired and turned into a mobile-first experience. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fortnite-creators-can-soon-make-star-wars-battlefront-style-islands/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Lets Players Choose Their Own Rewards
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite’s Chapter 7 Season 2 goes live today, asking players to either join Team Foundation or Team Ice King, and their decisions will affect the final reward.

After a two-week delay, players are finally receiving a brand-new season of Fortnite, Showdown. If you are just getting into it, here’s a complete breakdown of all the new weapons in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2, alongside the unvaulted and returning weapons. Now, check out how battle pass rewards work in this season.

Fortnite Showdown Season Changes the Final Reward Depending on Player Decisions

The latest Fortnite blog post reveals that in Chapter 7 Season 2, players will be able to choose between two teams: Team Foundation and Team Ice King. Once chosen, players have to win with their team to unlock new weapons, items, and rewards. Moreover, the team that emerges victorious towards the end of the season will have the decision to choose the final Battle Pass reward, which can either be Frost-Ready Foundation or Exalted Frost Ice King.

Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Brings Rivalry Gameplay and Arenas

Moreover, the latest season introduces rivalry gameplay, which lets players go head-to-head with others in the lobby. Winning these Rivalry matches provides players with points for their respective teams along with Rival Credits that can be used to purchase perks, weapons, and XP. Players will also get a new Ranked and Build-Only mode called Arenas on April 9, where they can either participate in a 1v1 boxfight or a 16-player round robin competition as a duo or solo.

In other news, here’s a guide on whether you should choose Team Ice King or Team Foundation in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2. Additionally, here are all the details on the launch week bonus and an overview of everything included. What are your thoughts on Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 allowing players to choose their rewards? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Pearl Abyss share price crashing after Crimson Desert reviews go live. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fortnite-chapter-7-season-2-lets-players-choose-their-own-rewards/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Weapons—All New & Unvaulted Weapons
item.content:encoded:
The long-awaited seasonal update has finally arrived in Fortnite, and Chapter 7 Season 2 significantly refreshes the loot pool with a series of both new and unvaulted weapons.

A two-week delay to the start of the new season made Chapter 7 Season 1 the third-longest in Fortnite history, but Epic Games is seemingly pulling out all the stops to make the long wait worthwhile.

For a full breakdown of the loot pool changes in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2, Insider Gaming has all the details you need.

All New Weapons & Equipment in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2

Epic Games describes the Chapter 7 Season 2 loot pool in Fortnite as ‘almost entirely refreshed,’ including a heap of new weapons.

The Vector 7 DMR, Chaos Reloader Shotgun, and Bouncing Boomstick could become staples of any player’s loadout this season, while the Skyline Deployer could be vital for securing the high ground in battles or in the closing stages of matches.

  • More Insider Gaming: Should You Choose Team Ice King or Team Foundation?

Meanwhile, the Ice King’s Gauntlets look to be the biggest item to track down early in the season, dropped by the Ice King at Frigid Fortress, so there could be some big battles ahead to add it to your arsenal.

The full list of new weapons and equipment found in Fortnite this season is below:

  • Vector 7 DMR
  • Chaos Reloader Shotgun
  • Bouncing Boomstick
  • Overdrive Grenade
  • Skyline Deployer
  • Ice King’s Gauntlets
  • The Foundation’s Rift Rifle
  • Seven Cluster Cannon
  • Seven Power Gloves
  • Slapperoni Pizza

All Unvaulted Weapons & Equipment in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2

Alongside the slew of new weapons and equipment in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2, some familiar names are returning to the loot pool.

The Twin Mag SMG is among the headliners, though the most eye-catching inclusion is the mention of Foundation-inspired versions of Exotic weapons from Chapter 6 Season 2.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Launch Week Bonus Rewards & How to Get Them

Additional weapons and equipment will be unvaulted as the season progresses, so keep an eye out for new additions coming to the loot pool.

The full list of unvaulted weapons and equipment, along with the returning items, in Fortnite this season is shown below:

  • Combat Assault Rifle
  • Nemesis AR
  • Chapter 1 Pistol
  • Thunder Burst SMG
  • Iron Pump Shotgun
  • Twin Hammer Shotguns
  • Twin Mag SMG
  • Mythic Goldfish
  • FlowBerry Mist Grenade (Zero Build only)
  • Shield Bubble Jr. (Zero Build Only)
  • Foundation-inspired versions of Chapter 6 Season 2 Exotics

Which weapon has caught your eye for Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2? Share your ideal loadouts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, read about the return to the Google Play Store, and check out the tease of Italian Brainrot characters.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fortnite-chapter-7-season-2-weapons/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: FaZe Fall To Narrow Loss in First Showing Without NEO
item.content:encoded:
FaZe lost 2-1 to Aurora in their opening match at BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026, their first showing without former coach Filip ‘NEO’ Kubski. Dominik ‘GruBy’ Swiderski is acting as the interim coach as FaZe search for a new vision for their Counter-Strike roster.

Despite the lack of a permanent coach, FaZe need to pick up a few results at BLAST or in open LANs to ensure their qualification for the IEM Cologne 2026 Major.

Aurora overcome FaZe despite jcobbb and Twistzz performances

Despite FaZe’s ongoing struggles, which saw the team regress significantly in 2025, the roster largely continued to have Aurora’s number in their meetings before Wednesday.

In truth, the series was a messy affair for both teams, laden with flubbed smokes, chaotic midrounds, and rounds lost to ecos.

Those conditions have usually suited FaZe in the past, and it seemed that once again they would benefit from a dogfight as they came up clutch in a tight Nuke contest to take the map 13-11.

Struggling young rifler, Jakub ‘jcobbb’ Pietruszewski, stood out on Nuke, but on Inferno, the Pole truly shone in an outstanding performance which saw him drop an 18/1/5 statline on the opening CT side.

However, even with jcobbb activated, FaZe could not get the read on where Aurora intended to end rounds, with the Turkish roster consistently breaking in to Inferno’s infamously hard to retake sites.

Aurora prevailed 13-8 on Inferno, shutting down FaZe’s T side before a streaky Dust 2 which saw Aurora start 5-0 on the T side before FaZe equalized the scoreline 6-6 at the half. Aurora won and converted the CT side pistol, before a streak of four out of five rounds for FaZe left the game in the balance.

Despite Russel ‘Twistzz’ Van Dulken’s best individual efforts, FaZe could not convert that streak into a map win, falling 13-10 as FaZe’s supporting cast fell flat.

The series once again highlighted the work needed in FaZe to modernize their Counter-Strike. While FaZe are known as the great entertainers, their style has begun to do more damage to themselves rather than the opponent.

Whoever succeeds NEO will have a lot of work to bring structure to a team that has often been defined by the absence of it.

Elsewhere on day one of BLAST Open Rotterdam, Falcons defeated NRG 2-0, NAVI beat B8 2-1, and FURIA completed a late-night contest, beating TYLOO 2-0 as Wednesday night turned into Thursday morning.

Who do you think should coach FaZe? Discuss that, and more, on Insider Gaming’s Discord server!

For more Counter-Strike news, see the drastic change to reloading in the latest update.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For March 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, March 19, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Fashion
  • Hint 2: Stage
  • Hint 3: Runway

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Supermodel
  • Grace
  • Strut
  • Glamour
  • Backstage
  • Menswear
  • Womenswear
  • Fashion
  • Runway

Today’s Contexto Answer For March 19, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Catwalk

A Catwalk is a narrow walkway, along which models often walk to display clothing. Also used as open bridges in industrial installations.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on March 20.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • NYT Connections

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android
